title: Paths to Superuser Privileges in UNIX
description: A flowchart illustrating the primary methods for a regular user to gain superuser (root) privileges on a UNIX system, including command usage and authentication flow.
relationships: User executes 'su -' to switch user, is prompted for root password, and gains a root shell if correct
User executes a command with 'sudo', is prompted for their own password (if not recently authenticated), and executes the command with root privileges if authorized in /etc/sudoers
Both methods provide elevated privileges but differ in scope and authentication requirements
